title: linaria aeruginea var. atrofusca
intro: Linaria aeruginea var. atrofusca, also known as the Purple Toadflax, is a perennial plant species within the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and ease congestion, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, Linaria aeruginea var. atrofusca is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
